reasoning: Qwen's unsafe findings are false positives; flagged scripts aren't executed during AUR build/install. Core package files (PKGBUILD, install hooks) show no malicious behavior.
disagreement_analysis: Qwen misinterpreted test/developer scripts as build risks; Deepseek correctly ignored non-packaging files.
coverage_issues: Qwen reviewed irrelevant test scripts (stats/probe_test/*.sh) not executed during packaging
false_positives: build-offline.sh: Not executed by PKGBUILD; standard offline build helper
upload.sh/schedule.sh: Test scripts unused in packaging; command injection concern irrelevant
probe_test.sh: Automated data upload is test behavior, not malicious exfiltration
learnings: Prioritize files referenced in PKGBUILD functions (prepare/build/package) and .install scripts
Improve context awareness: Ignore test/CI scripts unless invoked by packaging
Add validation: Cross-check script execution paths against PKGBUILD
Reduce false positives: Train models on benign patterns like offline build helpers
